Bill.title: Bill
Bill.description: Represents a single bill generated by a manager.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bill entity.

managerId.description: Reference to the Manager who created the bill. (Relationship: Manager 1:N Bill)

inCarat.description: The amount of carat that came in.

outCarat.description: The amount of carat that went out.

caratType.description: Type of carat (Small Carat or Big Carat).

rate.description: Rate per carat based on the carat type (17 for Small Carat, 20 for Big Carat).

totalAmount.description: Total amount due for the carat.

dueAmount.description: Amount remaining to be paid (Total Amount - Paid Amount).

paidTo.description: Name of the person to whom the payment was made (Gopal Dada, Yuvraj Dada, Suyash Dada, Gaju Dada).

dateTime.description: Date and time when the bill was generated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Manager entity.
Unique identifier for the Owner entity.

definition.description: Stores manager profiles. The 'managerId' is the Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: managerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the manager, corresponding to the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Stores bills created by each manager. Includes the denormalized 'managerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: managerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the manager who created the bill.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier for the bill.

definition.description: Documents in this collection indicate owner status. Existence of a document with the UID as the document ID grants owner privileges.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the owner, corresponding to the Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: Collection containing all bills.  This is for global access by the owner.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier for the bill.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support the Aanand Sagar Billing App, focusing on security, scalability, and ease of debugging. The core principle is Authorization Independence, achieved through denormalization to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. This enhances the atomicity of operations and simplifies debugging. Segregation of data based on access control requirements is also prioritized.

The structure uses path-based ownership for manager-specific data. The bills are stored within the manager's document to leverage Firestore's hierarchical nature for simplified security rules and efficient data retrieval.

**Authorization Independence:** The `managerId` is included in each bill document, enabling direct authorization checks without needing to query the `managers` collection. This ensures that access control decisions are self-contained within the bill document itself.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The segregation of bills under each manager ensures that list operations can be secured effectively.  The owner can view all bills because of an additional collection `/bills`. The manager views his own bills because of the `/managers/{managerId}/bills/{billId}` collection.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:** Consistent naming conventions are used throughout the structure, such as `managerId` and `billId`. The `dateTime` field provides explicit timestamp information for each bill.
